issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close functions do not update aria‑expanded on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened or closed the script only toggles CSS classes and the hidden attribute. The trigger button never receives an aria‑expanded state change, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons lack an initial aria‑expanded attribute (default false)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger receives aria‑haspopup but never receives an aria‑expanded value on page load, leaving the accessibility tree ambiguous.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑pressed on the switch element
issuesFound.explanation: The UI shows a visual on/off switch for annual vs. monthly billing, but the element never reflects its state via aria‑pressed, breaking AT expectations for a toggle control.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle is missing an explicit aria‑pressed initial value on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The switch element receives a role="switch" but never gets an initial aria‑pressed state, leaving the default undefined for assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced to screen‑reader users
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the off‑canvas menu changes focus and visibility but no live‑region message is emitted, so users relying on AT receive no feedback that the navigation drawer is now open or closed.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The menu’s visual state changes but no aria‑live announcement is made, leaving AT users unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Changing the billing period does not announce the new price state to AT users
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly, quarterly, or annual pricing the UI updates numbers, but no live‑region message is emitted, so screen‑reader users cannot perceive the change.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing a FAQ item updates the DOM but does not provide an aria‑live announcement, so AT users have no feedback.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS only and never receives aria‑hidden when closed (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is visually hidden by removing the open class, but because it remains in the accessibility tree ATs may still navigate into it. The spec requires aria‑hidden="true" when the drawer is closed.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen‑reader users (JS‑C)
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the numeric badge changes but no live‑region announcement is made, so AT users cannot know how many results remain.

fpTitles: Pricing period toggle does not update aria‑pressed on the switch element
Pricing period toggle is missing an explicit aria‑pressed initial value on page load

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing `aria-controls` on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button used to open/close the mobile navigation drawer is given `aria-expanded` via `setExpanded`, but no `aria-controls` attribute is set to reference the drawer element (`#nav-menu`). Assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the region it controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: `setExpanded(_trigger, true, _drawer);` → `setExpanded(_trigger, true, _drawer, 'nav-menu');` (or add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'nav-menu');` before the call).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: `openInstance` shows the dropdown panel and adds the open class, but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1582: after `inst.open = true;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not update `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: `closeInstance` hides the dropdown panel but does not set `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger, so the collapsed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1595: after `inst.open = false;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set `aria-expanded` on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the code adds CSS classes and animates the panel but never updates the trigger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, breaking the ARIA Disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2368: after `item.open = true;` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not clear `aria-expanded` on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Closing an FAQ item removes CSS classes and animates the panel but does not set `aria‑expanded="false"` on the trigger, leaving the state stale for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2382: after `item.open = false;` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons lack `aria-pressed` state
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`data-period="month"` etc.) are toggles that switch billing cycles, but the code never updates `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑checked`) to reflect the active period, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3064 (inside `_syncToggleState`): after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑toggle switch missing `aria-checked` (or `aria-pressed`)
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off billing toggle (`data-billing-toggle`) is given `role="switch"` but never updates `aria‑checked` when toggled, violating the ARIA Switch pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3020 (inside `_onToggleClick`): after `setPeriod(next);` add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(next === 'year'));`.

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` marks fields as invalid visually but only adds CSS classes; it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the input, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4472: after detecting an invalid result, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` (and ensure removal on valid).

issuesFound.title: Inline field validator does not clear `aria-invalid` on valid inputs (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error styling but does not remove the `aria-invalid` attribute, leaving the element incorrectly marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4479: on successful validation, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls` (duplicate of Issue 1 but for completeness of the rule)
issuesFound.explanation: The same `.nav-toggle` button lacks an explicit `aria-controls` reference to the drawer (`#nav-menu`). Without it, AT cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1245: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'nav-menu');` before calling `setExpanded`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack explicit `aria-controls` linking to the price display region
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`data-period`) control the visibility of price values in each plan card but no `aria-controls` attribute points to the region they affect, breaking the relationship for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3060 (in `setPeriod` after updating UI): for each period button, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'price-display-${planId}');` where the target element has that id.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload hide routine uses off‑screen positioning without `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When a file entry is hidden (e.g., after removal), the code removes the DOM element but for intermediate “hide” states it applies CSS that moves the element off‑screen rather than setting `display:none`. Because the element remains in the accessibility tree, it should also receive `aria-hidden="true"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5278 (inside `_hideEntry` before removing the element): add `setAttr(entry.rowEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`.

fpTitles: Pricing period buttons lack explicit `aria-controls` linking to the price display region

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions toggle the mobile navigation drawer but never set or update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `open()` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `close()` (or use `toggleAttr`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation button (`_trigger`) does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that references the ID of the navigation drawer (`_drawer`). Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix: After obtaining `_drawer`, ensure it has an ID (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer)`) and set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)` during initialization.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search component shows and hides the suggestion list, but the search `<input>` never receives an `aria-expanded` attribute reflecting the open/closed state of the listbox. This violates the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close()` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`_periodBtns`) are toggled with a visual `active` class, but they do not receive an `aria-pressed` attribute to convey their pressed state to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState()` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive))` for each button.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult()` marks fields with CSS classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs nor removes it on valid inputs. Assistive technologies cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult()`, when `!result.valid` call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid state
issuesFound.explanation: Even after a field becomes valid, the validator only removes error styling but does not clear the `aria-invalid` attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure that on a valid result `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` is executed (as part of the fix for Issue 6).

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list items do not update aria‑selected
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list renders `<li role="option">` elements, but when navigating with arrow keys the script only toggles a CSS class and does not update `aria-selected` on the active option. Screen readers cannot know which suggestion is highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: In `suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex()` set `setAttr(item, 'aria-selected', 'true')` for the active item and `removeAttr(item, 'aria-selected')` for others.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded update on programmatic open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open()` and `close()` functions correctly set `aria-expanded` on the trigger, but the `openAll()` and `closeAll()` helpers call `open(item)` / `close(item)` without first ensuring the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is updated, leading to inconsistent state when multiple items are opened programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `openAll()` and `closeAll()` invoke the same `open()` / `close()` paths that set `aria-expanded`, or explicitly call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'/'false')` within those helpers.

issuesFound.title: Pricing comparison table column headers missing aria‑controls linkage
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table uses `data-plan-head` attributes for column headers but does not provide `aria-controls` that reference the corresponding plan card IDs. Users cannot navigate from the header to the detailed plan.
issuesFound.fix: During `planComparisonHighlighter.init()`, for each header element add `setAttr(header, 'aria-controls', planId)` where `planId` is the ID of the matching plan card.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload zone does not set aria‑describedby for error messages
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation (`_uploadError`), an error message is announced via live region but the upload entry element does not receive an `aria-describedby` reference to that message, leaving AT users unaware of the specific error.
issuesFound.fix: In `_uploadError()` after creating the error message, call `setAttr(entry.rowEl, 'aria-describedby', errorEl.id)` where `errorEl` is the status element containing the error text.

issuesFound.title: Theme toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The theme switcher listens for clicks on elements with `[data-theme-toggle]` but does not set `aria-pressed` to indicate the current theme state.
issuesFound.fix: In `themeSwitcher.toggle()` and `themeSwitcher.set()`, after updating the theme, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnTheme === currentTheme))` for each toggle button.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden by _openInstance and _closeInstance, but these functions do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button (inst.trigger) to reflect the open/closed state. Assistive technology users cannot perceive the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _openInstance function: Add as first line of function body
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line _closeInstance function: Add as first line of function body (before the if (!inst.open) check)
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line markInvalid function: Add after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`
  `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked valid, the function removes the error description association but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. The field may still be reported as invalid by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line markValid function: Add before the closing brace of the function
  `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-controls set to reference the drawer element (_drawer). Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix:
Line init function: After `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);` and the null check, add
  `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line setPeriod function: At the end of the function (before the emit call), add
  `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved to the drawer or its first focusable element. Keyboard users remain on the trigger button and must navigate manually to the newly opened content, which may be confusing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open function: After `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });` add
  `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix:
Line close function: Before `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();` add
  `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ panels are expanded and collapsed dynamically, but the state change is not announced via a live region. While aria-expanded is set on the trigger, users may not realize the content has appeared or disappeared, especially if they activated the control via keyboard and the content is below the viewport.
issuesFound.fix:
Line open function: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });` add
  `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);`
  Line close function: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });` add
  `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch clear action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the search input is cleared via the clear button, the suggestions panel closes but no announcement is made confirming the action. Screen reader users may not realize the search has been cleared and the suggestions dismissed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line clear function: After `_searchState.query = '';` add
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchClearing || 'Search cleared');`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves to the skip link, but no announcement confirms the action to screen reader users. Users may not understand what happened to the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line _onClick function: After `setTimeout(() => { returnEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }); }, 300);` add
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not moved on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved to the drawer or its first focusable element, leaving keyboard users at the trigger button instead of inside the new modal context.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open() before emit(): `const first = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (first) first.focus(); else _drawer.focus();`

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismiss without pause
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications auto-dismiss after a timeout without a mechanism to pause, stop, or reset the timer on hover or focus, violating WCAG 2.2.1 for users who need more time to read the content.
issuesFound.fix: Add to show() after creating toast: `on(toast, 'mouseenter', () => clearTimeout(timerId)); on(toast, 'mouseleave', () => { if (dur > 0) timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); }); on(toast, 'focusin', () => clearTimeout(timerId)); on(toast, 'focusout', () => { if (dur > 0) timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu panel is shown and hidden by _openInstance and _closeInstance, but these functions do not toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250 (_openInstance): Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 2260 (_closeInstance): Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion items are expanded and collapsed via the open() and close() functions, but neither function sets aria-expanded on the trigger button. This violates the WAI-ARIA Accordion pattern and prevents screen reader users from knowing the state of each panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2800 (open function): Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 2820 (close function): Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tab buttons toggle the visible product cards but do not set aria-pressed to indicate the active filter. This prevents assistive technologies from identifying which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500 (_syncTabAriaStates): Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop after isActive is determined.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button triggers missing Enter/Space keyboard handlers
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items may use non-button elements (divs or spans) with role="button" as triggers, but the _onKeydown handler only manages Arrow, Home, and End keys. It does not handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the panel, making the accordion unusable for keyboard-only users when the trigger is not a native button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850 (_onKeydown): Add case KEYS.ENTER: case KEYS.SPACE: e.preventDefault(); toggle(item); break;

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent focus trap does not return focus to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is closed, focus is not returned to the button that opened it (or the skip link if auto-shown). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and disorients keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100 (_hideBanner): Before removeElement, add: const trigger = qs('[data-cookie-prefs]') || qs('.skip-link'); if (trigger) trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, focus is not returned to the hamburger menu button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the page focus order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170 (close function): Before emit, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu close does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed (via Escape or outside click), focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. This breaks the expected keyboard interaction pattern for disclosure widgets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2260 (_closeInstance): Add: if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) inst.trigger.focus();

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid helper function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200 (markInvalid): Add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator renders errors without setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, _renderResult calls srUtils.markInvalid which does not set aria-invalid. Additionally, the function itself does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields, preventing assistive technologies from identifying invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1300 (_renderResult): In the else branch (when !result.valid), add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The form validation iterates through fields and validates them, but relies on inlineValidator.validate which does not set aria-invalid. Invalid fields are not marked with aria-invalid="true", so screen reader users cannot identify which fields need correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3000 (_validateAll): After const result = inlineValidator.validate(field), add: if (result && !result.valid) setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation does not set aria-invalid on email field
issuesFound.explanation: When email validation fails, the code calls inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl) but does not explicitly set aria-invalid="true" on the email input. The error is visually shown but not conveyed to assistive technologies via aria-invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050 (_onSubmit): After const result = inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl), add: if (!result.valid) setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element. Screen reader users cannot identify which element is controlled by the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100 (init function): After _drawer is assigned, add: if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Screen readers announce the button without indicating that it controls a collapsed navigation panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2100 (init function): After registering elements, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. The register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but omits the initial expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2200 (register): After setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId), add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price values update visually but no announcement is made to screen reader users about the price change or the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2350 (setPeriod): After _updatePlanCard(plan, period) loop, add: liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible content significantly, but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu is now open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150 (open): Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); Line 2170 (close): Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus opening and closing are not announced to screen reader users. This prevents users from knowing when the dropdown content is available for interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250 (_openInstance): Add liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent} submenu expanded`); Line 2260 (_closeInstance): Add liveRegion.announce(`${inst.trigger.textContent} submenu collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ panels expanding and collapsing are not announced. Although the aria-expanded attribute should be present (see Issue 3), the content change itself should be announced for clarity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2800 (open): Add if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`); Line 2820 (close): Add if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Statistical counters animate visually when scrolling into view, but the changing numbers are not announced to screen reader users, denying them access to the statistical information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3300 (_animate): In onComplete callback, add: liveRegion.announce(`Statistic updated to ${el.textContent}`);
